lastRunSummary: Built Game 34: Dust Devil Dance - generative music sandbox, draw paths for dust devils, pentatonic oscillators, landmark audio effects, resonance meter, 25 tests pass.

gameQueue.title: Snake Canyon
gameQueue.concept: First-person maze-crawl: navigate a procedurally-tiled canyon network by torch, with a rattlesnake that can hear your footsteps. Move silently by tapping slowly and deliberately; rush and it strikes. Dynamic audio cue system - snake rattle volume and direction reveal proximity before visibility does.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First procedurally-generated level layout in series, first game where audio is the primary navigation sense (not vision), deepest audio-spatial design since Moonshine Run

gameQueue.title: Rodeo Queen
gameQueue.concept: Skill-chain combo game: player performs barrel racing, roping, and trick riding events in sequence. Each event uses a different gesture pattern; chaining events without errors builds a multiplier. Live crowd-energy meter rises and falls based on combo streaks. First combo-chain structure in the series.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First multi-discipline skill-chain structure in series, deepest combo/streak system since Pony Express, first game with distinct sub-game phases sharing a single energy meter

gameQueue.title: Tumbledown Town
gameQueue.concept: Physics demolition puzzle: a corrupt sheriff's crooked saloon is propped up by rickety supports. Tap to knock out supports one at a time, guiding the collapse to crush the wanted targets below while sparing the innocents. First physics-based demolition puzzle in the series.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First Verlet/impulse physics structure simulation in series, first demolition puzzle mechanic, first game where the goal is controlled collapse rather than survival or construction
